It does need to be about you, but it can't be in a way that doesn't now make them want to be hooked to you. And the last thing I'll say is, again, I would give different advice about that to people not trying to transcend a certain level of status for a certain level of income. And so, if we were doing a regular marketing seminar and talking about copywriting, you would get a different answer than the answer I just gave you. Hi, my name is Lars from Denmark. So, about this being appalled business, are these techniques, strategies, tactics, are they universal? Or is there a limitation, or is there some audiences that wouldn't work? No, there's, well, first of all, there are always people eager to be appalled. So, there is always an audience that will respond that way. Given that that is the case, there is always now the other part of that audience, which tends to be a minority, by the way, but they're the most valuable who respond exactly the opposite to the majority. So, when the majority is sufficiently appalled, there's a minority you are resonating with like there's no tomorrow. So, for many years, I've outgrown it in terms of its ability to pay me what I want to be paid. But for many years, I did a lot of business in the National Speakers Association market. Association members and non-association members, but largely association, consulting clients. And I would say that a good, if I stood up and spoke somewhere, a local chapter, a seminar, they never let me at the convention, but well once. They were regretting it though before I even did it, which is kind of funny. So, it wasn't a problem after the fact, the problem was before the fact. But I would say a good 80-85% really appalled and offended and annoyed. But the opposite person had been waiting to hear it and now is like drawn like this. And no, I don't think there's a place, I don't believe there's a place where this doesn't apply. So, if you watch our politics at all, consider Sarah Palin as polarizing as can be. So in upstate New York about two months ago, which is largely a democratic area and it's largely a liberal population, she pulled 22,000 people to a rally. They didn't come from upstate New York, they came from every place just because she's going to be there. That's not going to get her elected president because you actually need nearly a majority to make that happen. Well, because we have an electoral college system, you don't need a majority, but you need nearly a majority. So it's not going to get her elected president, but it will make her a shitload of money. Because the vast majority who are repulsed and repelled, then there's this minority over here who are drawn to her as if she was magnetized. So it really depends on what you want to do, right? But if she wants to make a crapload of money, she's got it about right for the next two years. In terms of celebrityizing oneself, how do you feel about doing things like newsletter guru, king of copy, I think you're the millionaire maker with a caricature. I was thinking about becoming anointing myself, the Sultan of Seller Finance. Well, look, there's an issue of cute, not cute, and that's sort of market sensitive. But back to the self-anointment and the creation of that self-made bubble in which you live. You'll find more do than don't, more have than haven't, and therefore if we're going to play the odds, we would. And there's sort of the casual moniker thing, like millionaire maker, yada yada. The more important is sort of the, what do you want to be known for to a specific market, and that reinforced again and again and again and again and again. So now if you go into your depth material, I'll give you, and you look at Ogilvy, you look at arms, you look at every single one of them. You will find that they have carved out that sort of a position, maybe not cutesy. In some cases, cutesy, I mean Ogilvy walking out of the cape, right? But they have carved out that for themselves and have used it at least for a period of time. Because people need a way to have you in their head, right, that has differentiation, that has separation. And so generally speaking, I'm all for it.
